openings.name: Pirc Defense
openings.moves: 1.e4 d6  2.d4 Nf6  3.Nc3 g6  4.Nf3 Bg7  5.Be2 O-O  6.O-O c6  7.h3 Qc7  8.Be3 b5  9.a3 Nbd7  10.Qd2 e5
openings.plan: Pirc setup: ...d6, ...Nf6, ...g6, ...Bg7, castle, then hit the center with ...c6/...e5. Keep the king safe first; do not drift into random Modern move orders.

openings.name: Englund Gambit
openings.moves: 1.d4 e5  2.dxe5 Nc6  3.Nf3 Qe7  4.Bf4 Qb4+  5.Bd2 Qxb2  6.Bc3 Bb4  7.Qd2 Bxc3  8.Qxc3 Qc1#
openings.plan: Force tactical chaos, target the b2/c3 weakness. If White gets greedy (6.Bc3?), the trap mates: ...Bb4, ...Bxc3, ...Qc1#.

openings.name: Colle–Zukertort System
openings.moves: 1.d4 d5  2.Nf3 Nf6  3.e3 e6  4.Bd3 c5  5.b3 Nc6  6.Bb2 Bd6  7.O-O O-O  8.Nbd2 b6  9.Ne5 Bb7  10.f4 Qc7
openings.plan: e3 + b3 + Bb2 fianchetto, dark bishop stays home (NO early Bf4). Castle, plant the knight on e5, then f4 and a kingside pawn storm / Bxh7 attack.

openings.name: Four Knights Game
openings.moves: 1.e4 e5  2.Nf3 Nc6  3.Nc3 Nf6  4.Bb5 Bb4  5.O-O O-O  6.d3 d6  7.Bg5 Bxc3  8.bxc3 Qe7  9.Re1 Nd8  10.d4 Ne6
openings.plan: Sound classical Four Knights (Spanish, 4.Bb5): develop both knights, castle, then the d4 central break. No material sacrifice — holds up against accurate / engine defence, unlike the gambits.

openings.name: Caro-Kann (bench)
openings.moves: 1.e4 c6  2.d4 d5  3.Nc3 dxe4  4.Nxe4 Bf5  5.Ng3 Bg6  6.h4 h6  7.Nf3 Nd7
openings.plan: Candidate vs 1.e4: solid pawn chain, trade light bishops early, aim for a safe ...c5/...e6 structure. Studying — not yet committed.

openings.name: London System (bench)
openings.moves: 1.d4 d5  2.Nf3 Nf6  3.Bf4 e6  4.e3 Bd6  5.Bg3 O-O  6.Bd3 b6  7.Nbd2 Bb7
openings.plan: Candidate vs 1.d4: early Bf4 (contrast with the committed Colle-Zukertort, which forbids Bf4). Studying alternative setup.

principles: Blunder check — 3 candidate moves before every move
10–15 tactics per day, accuracy over speed
Opening principles ≥ lines: center, development, castle
Review every loss — find the eval-flip move
One opening per side, played always
